channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meet the mad scientist of headphones, whose wild designs electrify Reddit
item.description: The standard shape for big, chunky headphones has long been a rainbow with Moon Pies at either end. No rule exists, however, forcing audio companies to keep making them that way. Philip Kaplan, the tech entrepreneur behind Tiny Letter and DistroKid, has been proving as much all year.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Are your smart home cameras spying on you? Study reveals shocking data grabs
item.description: A new study says outdoor security camera apps are among the biggest collectors of user data, including phone numbers, payment details and precise location.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Dark Side of Health Optimization: How Smartwatches and Apps Can Corrupt Our Relationship with Exercise
item.description: A climate policy consultant shares their personal experience with the dark side of health optimization, how smartwatches and apps can corrupt our relationship with exercise, and the importance of being present in physical activity.
item.dc:creator: Cate Twining-Ward, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft backed a tiny hardware startup that just launched its first AI processor that does inference without GPU or expensive HBM memory and a key Nvidia partner is collaborating with it
item.description: Microsoft-backed d-Matrix launches Corsair, an AI processor offering efficient inference, GPU independence, and support for generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Android 15's new Private Space feature puts an end to app snooping
item.description: Android 15's latest feature, Private Space, is like a private vault helping to keep your personal or sensitive apps away from prying eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors
item.description: Learn how rich text editors help government agencies ensure compliance with data security, accessibility, and document standardization. The post Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here are the key tech, workforce provisions in the 2025 intel authorization bill
item.description: The latest authorization bill pushes the intelligence community to boost many of its workforce and tech efforts, including its public-private talent exchange. The post Here are the key tech, workforce provisions in the 2025 intel authorization bill first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Fake AI-generated reviews are all over the internet, research shows
item.description: The emergence of generative artificial intelligence tools that allow people to efficiently produce novel and detailed online reviews with almost no work has put merchants, service providers and consumers in uncharted territory, watchdog groups and researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company
